cli.welcome.body:
[bold cyan]🚛 FLEET SIZING OPTIMIZER[/bold cyan]

Interactive logistics fleet planning with what-if scenario analysis.
Runs lane-by-lane, weighted-cycle, and MILP optimization.
cli.summary.title: [bold]Problem Summary:[/bold]
cli.summary.scale_warning: [yellow]⚠  Large network ({n_cp} CPs, {n_t} terminals) — Scope 4.3 MILP may take up to 5 minutes.[/yellow]
cli.confirm_baseline: 🚀 [bold]Ready to run baseline analysis across all 3 scopes?[/bold]
cli.milp.infeasible_header: [bold yellow]⚠  MILP Infeasible — {n} node(s) exceed their monthly capacity[/bold yellow]
cli.milp.infeasible_body:
[dim]  The solver cannot satisfy all demand within the node capacity limits below.
  Scopes 4.1 and 4.2 are unaffected — they do not enforce capacity constraints.[/dim]
cli.milp.infeasible_row_cp: • {name}: demand [bold]{demand:,.0f}[/bold] t/mo  vs capacity {cap:,.0f} t/mo  ([red]+{over:,.0f} t over[/red])
cli.milp.infeasible_row_terminal: • {name}: demand [bold]{demand:,.0f}[/bold] t/mo  vs capacity {cap:,.0f} t/mo  ([red]+{over:,.0f} t over[/red])
cli.milp.infeasible_hint: [dim]  → Fix: increase capacity in terminals-specification.xlsx, or run a what-if scenario with cp_capacity / terminal_capacity.[/dim]
cli.baseline.infeasible_singular: [bold yellow]⚠ {n} route could not be served within the current shift hours and must be reviewed[/bold yellow]
cli.baseline.infeasible_plural: [bold yellow]⚠ {n} routes could not be served within the current shift hours and must be reviewed[/bold yellow]
cli.comparison.cost_note: [dim]  4.3 variable cost reflects LP trip mix (round-trip / one-way / repo); 4.1/4.2 assume round-trip only.[/dim]
cli.comparison.infeasible_singular: [bold yellow]⚠ {n} route can no longer be served within the shift hours under this scenario — review before implementing[/bold yellow]
cli.comparison.infeasible_plural: [bold yellow]⚠ {n} routes can no longer be served within the shift hours under this scenario — review before implementing[/bold yellow]
cli.driver.title: [bold cyan]🚗 Driver Policy — Current Values[/bold cyan]
cli.nodes.title: [bold cyan]📍 Network Nodes[/bold cyan]
cli.relocation.already_optimal: [bold green]✓ Current allocation is already optimal — no relocation improves the fleet.[/bold green]
cli.relocation.saving: Estimated fleet reduction (continuous relaxation): [bold green]−{saving:.2f} trucks  (−{pct:.1f}%)[/bold green]
cli.costs.title: [bold cyan]Truck Operational Cost Structure[/bold cyan]
cli.costs.variable_header: [bold]Variable Costs[/bold]  [dim](applied per km driven — all trip types)[/dim]
cli.costs.fixed_header: [bold]Fixed Costs[/bold]  [dim](per truck per month, regardless of utilisation)[/dim]
cli.whatif.title: [bold cyan]🎯 What-If Scenario Types[/bold cyan]
cli.whatif.s1_subtitle: Overtime, shift hours, working days per month
cli.whatif.s2_ex2: • "Loaded speed drops 10% due to road works"
cli.whatif.s3_subtitle: Variable costs (fuel, tires, maintenance) and fixed costs
cli.whatif.s4_subtitle: Capacity, unload time, and cargo volume
cli.whatif.s5_subtitle: Parameter change + demand relocation in one step
cli.whatif.s5_body1: Include the words [bold]relocate[/bold] or [bold]optimize[/bold] to run the demand
cli.whatif.s5_body2: relocation after applying the parameter change. The result compares
cli.whatif.s5_body3: the combined outcome directly against the original baseline.
cli.whatif.s5_ex1: • "Set fuel to $3.80/km and relocate the volume"
cli.whatif.s5_ex2: • "Availability drops to 80% — can cargo relocation offset the fleet increase?"
cli.whatif.s5_ex3: • "Add 1 hour of overtime and optimize demand distribution"
cli.whatif.tip1: • Type [bold]/driver[/bold] to see current driver policy and cost values before querying
cli.whatif.tip2: • Type [bold]/list[/bold] to see all nodes, capacities, and current demand
cli.whatif.tip3: • Type [bold]/relocate[/bold] to run standalone demand relocation on the current baseline
cli.whatif.tip4: • Type [bold]/operational-costs[/bold] to see the full cost breakdown

nl.api_key.set_key: Set your Claude API key before running what-if analysis:

nl.no_scenario: [yellow]Could not identify a clear scenario. Try rephrasing or type [bold]/whatif[/bold] for examples.[/yellow]
nl.running_relocation: [dim]Running demand relocation on the modified scenario...[/dim]
nl.complete: [bold green]✓ What-if analysis complete. {n} scenario(s) explored.[/bold green]
nl.analyst.rating_prompt: [dim]How useful was this guidance?  1 = off-target   2 = useful   3 = excellent[/dim]
nl.analyst.note_prompt: [dim]Add a note to analyst memory? (corrections, constraints, priorities — press Enter to skip)[/dim]
nl.relocate.confirm: Apply relocation and run full scenario analysis?
nl.relocate.default_label: Optimal demand relocation across collection points
nl.help.panel_title: [bold cyan]📚 HELP — Fleet Sizing What-If Analysis[/bold cyan]
nl.help.list: [cyan]/list[/cyan]                Shows all collection points and terminals
nl.help.driver: [cyan]/driver[/cyan]              Shows current driver policy and truck parameters
nl.help.whatif: [cyan]/whatif[/cyan]              Shows what types of questions you can explore
nl.help.relocate: [cyan]/relocate[/cyan]            Optimises fleet by relocating demand across collection points
nl.help.baseline: [cyan]/baseline[/cyan]            Shows the baseline fleet sizing results
nl.help.opcosts: [cyan]/operational-costs[/cyan]   Shows the truck cost structure and how rates are applied
nl.help.analyst: [cyan]/planning-analyst[/cyan]    Synthesises all saved scenarios into a strategic report
nl.help.network: [cyan]/network[/cyan]             Show network topology and demand heatmap
nl.onboarding.panel_title: [bold cyan]👋 Welcome — How This Tool Works[/bold cyan]
nl.onboarding.lane_by_lane: [bold]Lane-by-Lane:[/bold] calculates how many trucks you need if every route runs independently, never sharing a truck with another route.
nl.onboarding.weighted_cycle: [bold]Weighted Avg Cycle:[/bold] assumes trucks are shared across routes during the day, weighted by how much volume each route carries. This is the number your fleet should actually be sized to in a well-run operation.
nl.onboarding.milp: [bold]MILP[/bold] validates the Weighted Avg Cycle by building a real day-by-day schedule across all routes and terminals. When both fleet numbers agree, your target is not just an estimate — it is confirmed. That agreement is what makes this analysis reliable. MILP also produces its own operational cost, which may differ from the Weighted Avg Cycle cost: the schedule optimizer allocates trips differently from the cycle-time average, so use the MILP cost as the more precise figure when both are available. Start with [cyan]/baseline[/cyan] and ask your first what-if question.
cli.network.panel_title: [bold cyan]Network Topology[/bold cyan] — {n_cp} Collection Points → {n_t} Terminals
main.excel_hint: [yellow]Make sure all required Excel files are in the data/ directory.[/yellow]
